title: Client Onboarding Process Overview
content: New client onboarding takes 60–90 days and covers six workstreams: Actions (configuring health task catalog for the client), Branding (logo, color scheme, app customization), Data Files (eligibility file format and SFTP setup), Earning Cap (configuring per-member reward limits per contract), Redemptions (enabling reward redemption channels — PrizeOut, sweepstakes, retail), and Reporting (setting up client-specific LENS dashboard views). Each workstream has a milestone tracked in the Implementation Jira board. The Implementation Director owns the overall timeline.

title: Client Health Score Framework
content: Each strategic client is assigned a quarterly health score (Green / Yellow / Red). Inputs: Active Member Rate (target >40%), Reward Attach Rate (target >60%), Redemption Rate (target >30%), Case Volume Trend (increasing = negative signal), and Renewal Status (12+ months out = Green, 6–12 = Yellow, <6 = Red). CS Executives review health scores monthly. A Yellow score triggers a check-in call. A Red score triggers an executive escalation plan within 5 business days.

title: Renewal Process and Timeline
content: Contract renewals are initiated 12 months before expiry. Steps: 1) CS Executive schedules Executive Business Review (EBR) with client — 12 months out. 2) Legal drafts renewal SOW — 9 months out. 3) Pricing review with finance — 8 months out. 4) Client signs or negotiates — 6 months out. 5) Countersigned contract stored in the Contracts SharePoint folder. 6) CRM Account record updated with new contract dates. Renewals going past the 6-month mark without signature are escalated to the VP of Client Success.

title: Statement of Work (SOW) — Standard Commitments
content: Standard SOW commitments included in all client contracts: 99.9% platform uptime SLA (measured monthly, excluding planned maintenance), T1 case resolution within 1 business day, T2 case resolution within 3 business days, monthly performance reporting delivered by the 10th of the following month, annual Executive Business Review, and data file processing within 4 hours of SFTP delivery. Custom commitments (e.g., dedicated implementation manager, custom reporting) are noted in the SOW Addendum and tracked in the client's CRM Account record.

title: Executive Business Review (EBR) Template
content: The EBR is a quarterly or annual review with client leadership. Standard agenda: Platform performance vs SLA (uptime, case resolution), member engagement metrics (active rate, attach rate, redemption rate), health action completion trends, upcoming roadmap items relevant to the client, and open issues / risk items. Data for the EBR is pulled from LENS by the CS Executive one week before. The EBR deck template is in the CS SharePoint under Templates > EBR. Always personalize the cover slide with the client's branding.

title: New Client Launch Checklist
content: Pre-launch checklist (must be complete before go-live): eligibility file received and processed without quarantine errors, all tenant config fields populated and verified, reward earning rules tested end-to-end in staging, card issuance tested for at least 5 synthetic members, LENS dashboard configured and client-accessible, T1 agent training completed and call scripts approved, client communication plan approved (launch email, app store listing). Sign-off required from: Implementation Director, VP Engineering, and CS Executive. Checklist is tracked in the Implementation Jira epic.

title: Client Escalation Protocol
content: When a client raises a critical issue (P1 equivalent): 1) CS Executive acknowledges within 1 hour. 2) Creates a Jira escalation ticket linked to the client Account in CRM. 3) Loops in relevant team lead (Engineering, Data, or Ops). 4) Updates client every 2 hours until resolved. 5) Provides root cause summary within 24 hours of resolution. 6) Schedules a debrief call if the issue lasted more than 4 hours. All client escalations are reviewed at the weekly CS leadership meeting.

title: Implementation Configuration — Earning Cap Setup
content: The earning cap defines the maximum reward dollars a member can earn per period (monthly or annual) per the client contract. Configured in the tenant_config table field reward_earning_cap_cents and cap_period (monthly/annual). Default cap is $5.00/month unless the SOW specifies otherwise. The Implementation team sets this during onboarding and it requires sign-off from the CS Executive before activation. Changes to earning caps post-launch must go through a change-request process and are logged in the tenant_config_audit table.
